q: During the ovarian cycle, which hormone triggers ovulation?
options: Progesterone surge from the corpus luteum on day 14
A sharp rise in estrogen causes a surge in LH from the anterior pituitary, triggering ovulation
FSH from the hypothalamus directly causes the follicle to rupture
Human chorionic gonadotropin (hCG) peaks and stimulates the dominant follicle to release the oocyte
explanation: Rising estrogen from the maturing follicle triggers a positive feedback LH surge from the anterior pituitary around day 14, causing the follicle to rupture and release the secondary oocyte.
